cells-html-converter.error: System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
cells-html-converter.root_cause: Generated code calls HtmlConverter.Process(null, null) to demonstrate the options overload, causing NullReferenceException
cells-html-converter.fix: Remove the second overload call. Use only Process(templateFile, resultFile).

cells-spreadsheet-locker.error: Aspose.Cells.CellsException: No input has been specified for the process.
cells-spreadsheet-locker.root_cause: Generated code creates empty LowCodeLoadOptions without setting InputFile, then calls Process(loadOptions, saveOptions, ...). LowCodeLoadOptions requires InputFile to be set.
cells-spreadsheet-locker.fix: Remove extra overload calls. Use only Process(templateFile, resultFile, openPassword, writePassword).

LowCodeLoadOptions.rule: MUST set either InputFile or InputStream before passing to any Process method

LowCodeSaveOptions.xml_summary: Options for saving generated results in low code way.
LowCodeSaveOptions.rule: MUST set either OutputFile or OutputStream before passing to any Process method

HtmlConverter.reason: Simple overload works for standard file-to-file conversion

SpreadsheetLocker.simple: Process(templateFile: string, resultFile: string, openPassword: string, writePassword: string)
SpreadsheetLocker.options_basic: Process(loadOptions, saveOptions, openPassword: string, writePassword: string)
SpreadsheetLocker.options_full: Process(loadOptions, saveOptions, openPassword, writePassword, workbookPassword, workbookType)
SpreadsheetLocker.reason: Simple overload works for standard lock-with-password use case

key_insight: ALL Cells LowCode workflow roots have a simple string-path overload AND an options-based overload. The generated examples MUST use ONLY ONE overload per example — the simplest one. Do NOT demonstrate multiple overloads in a single example.
rule_for_llm: Call exactly ONE overload of each LowCode API. Prefer the string-path overload. If you must use options, set LowCodeLoadOptions.InputFile and LowCodeSaveOptions.OutputFile before calling Process().
